of_concept: mackey-topology-quotient-subspace

source_section: "4"
---

**Proof.** Apply Theorem 4.1 with $\mathfrak{S}_1'$ and $\mathfrak{S}_2'$ taken to be the saturated families generated by all convex, circled, weakly compact subsets of the respective duals. For the quotient assertion, use (c) $\Rightarrow$ (d) of Theorem 4.1. For the metrizable subspace assertion, use (a) $\Rightarrow$ (b) together with the fact that a subspace topology of a metrizable topology is metrizable. The conclusion that every separated quotient of a Mackey space is a Mackey space follows directly; that every metrizable subspace of a Mackey space is a Mackey space follows since metrizable subspaces inherit the Mackey topology. $\square$
